---
title: "ISSS608 – Visual Analytics & Applications"
toc: false
page-layout: full
title-block: false
---

::: {.homepage-shell}

::: {.homepage-intro}
<div class="intro-text">
  <p class="intro-label">SMU MITB · COURSE PORTFOLIO</p>
  <p class="intro-summary">
    This website documents my learning journey in ISSS608 through three main components:
    guided hands-on exercises, take-home assignments, and the final group project.
    Together, they reflect my development from foundational visualisation techniques
    to interactive analytical storytelling using R, Quarto, and Shiny.
  </p>
</div>
:::

## Course Portfolio Overview

This course portfolio is organised into three parts:

::: {.grid-section}
::: {.feature-card}
### Hands-on Exercise

This section captures the guided practical exercises completed throughout the course.

It includes work on data visualisation fundamentals, statistical graphics, geospatial methods,
interactive techniques, and dashboard-related analytical workflows in R.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Take-home Exercise

This section presents the individual take-home assignments, which place stronger emphasis on
problem framing, design choices, interpretation, and analytical storytelling.

These exercises demonstrate how visualisation can support both exploration and communication.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Group Project

This section links to the final group project developed for the course:
**COFINFAD: Colombian Fintech Customer Analytics Dashboard**.

The project integrates analytical design, interactivity, and storytelling through a web-based
visual analytics application.

[View Group Project Website](https://elliolee.github.io/ISSS608_VAA_ColombianFintech-project-website/){.project-link target="_blank"}
:::
:::

## Learning Focus

Across the semester, my work focused on five main areas:

- **Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)** for understanding structure, variation, and anomalies in data
- **Statistical graphics design** for clear and effective communication
- **Interactive visualisation** for user-driven exploration
- **Analytical storytelling** for turning results into meaningful narratives
- **Reproducible workflows in R and Quarto** for transparent and well-documented analysis

## Navigation Guide

Use the top navigation bar to browse the three course components:

- **Hands-on Exercise** for weekly guided practice
- **Take-home Exercise** for individual analytical assignments
- **Group Project** for the final project website

## Reflection

This portfolio reflects not only the outputs produced in the course, but also my evolving understanding
of visual analytics as a combination of analytical reasoning, visual design, interactivity, and structured communication.

:::
